subtopics.subtopic_description: Educational background or training
notes: Holds a Bachelor’s Degree, likely in Film, Media Production, or a related field
Completed coursework in directing, film production, and media studies
Attended workshops on advertising production and commercial filmmaking
Received on-the-job training in script breakdown and set management

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific job title and role description
notes: Works as a Director and Producer for commercials
Responsible for translating client scripts into visual concepts
Manages all aspects of production, from pre-visualization to shoot-day execution
Acts as the main liaison between the advertising agency, talent, and crew

subtopics.subtopic_description: Current industry or sector (e.g., tech, finance, manufacturing)
notes: Works in the Media and Entertainment sector
Specializes in advertising and commercial production
Collaborates closely with advertising agencies and creative teams

subtopics.subtopic_description: Company size and environment
notes: Typically operates within small to mid-sized production companies
Often works in dynamic, project-based environments
Regularly collaborates with freelance crew and agency teams

subtopics.subtopic_description: Type of business or market segment
notes: Focuses on creating short-form video content for commercial advertising
Targets clients in consumer goods, retail, and services sectors
Works on both B2B and B2C advertising projects

subtopics.subtopic_description: Duration/years of experience in current role
notes: Has over 10 years of experience as a producer and director
Spent the majority of career in commercial and advertising production
Progressed from junior roles to senior creative leadership

subtopics.subtopic_description: Professional seniority or career level
notes: Holds a senior-level position with significant creative authority
Makes key decisions regarding production design and execution
Mentors junior team members and guides project vision

subtopics.subtopic_description: Primary job responsibilities and regular daily tasks
notes: Oversees script interpretation and visual storyboarding
Coordinates with clients (advertising agencies) to align on vision
Approves wardrobe, props, and set design in collaboration with stylists and designers
Directs talent and crew on shoot days
Ensures production stays on schedule and within budget

subtopics.subtopic_description: Approximate proportion of time spent on core activities
notes: Storyboarding and script breakdown: ~25%
Client and agency meetings: ~20%
Crew and talent coordination: ~20%
Shoot day preparation and oversight: ~25%
Administrative follow-up (emails, scheduling): ~10%

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of autonomy and scope of decision-making in the role
notes: Exercises high autonomy in creative and logistical decisions
Has final say on visual interpretation and execution on set
Balances client requirements with production constraints
Delegates technical tasks to department leads but oversees all aspects

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that feel easiest or most natural to perform
notes: Storyboarding and visualizing scenes based on scripts
Communicating creative ideas to the agency and crew
Providing direction to talent during rehearsals and shoots

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks perceived as most challenging or complex
notes: Managing last-minute script or client-driven changes
Resolving unforeseen technical issues on set (e.g., equipment failure)
Balancing creative vision with client expectations and budget constraints

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are repetitive, data-heavy, or suitable for automation
notes: Coordinating shoot schedules and call sheets
Managing large volumes of production-related emails
Compiling and tracking wardrobe and props lists

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are most enjoyable or engaging versus those that feel boring or tedious
notes: Finds most enjoyment in creative tasks like storyboarding and directing talent
Enjoys collaborating with the art department to design sets and visuals
Finds repetitive administrative tasks (emails, scheduling) tedious

subtopics.subtopic_description: Common pain points or inefficiencies in completing tasks
notes: Difficulty in tracking communication threads across multiple email chains
Occasional misalignment between agency vision and production possibilities
Inefficiencies caused by last-minute changes to scripts or shoot plans

subtopics.subtopic_description: How enjoyment, skill level, and productivity relate to one another
notes: Most productive and effective when working on tasks that align with creative strengths
Enjoyment increases when collaborating with skilled, experienced teams
Skill level in storyboarding and direction correlates with higher project success rates

subtopics.subtopic_description: Attitude towards learning new technologies and tools
notes: Open and proactive about adopting new technologies when they enhance workflow
Sees value in learning tools that streamline creative or administrative processes
Enjoys experimenting with new software for storyboarding or editing

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived adaptability to new software/methods
notes: Adapts quickly to industry-standard editing and production tools
Familiar with integrating new digital solutions into established workflows
Comfortable seeking out tutorials or peer support for more complex tools

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness to invest time in tech training
notes: Willing to invest personal time in learning tools with clear professional benefits
Attends occasional webinars or workshops on production technology
Prefers hands-on learning over lengthy theoretical training

subtopics.subtopic_description: Motivations or barriers to learning new tech (e.g., workload, relevance)
notes: Motivated by the potential to save time or improve creative output
Barriers include high workload during production cycles and low perceived relevance of some tools
More likely to adopt tech recommended by trusted peers or visible in industry trends

subtopics.subtopic_description: Influence of peers or management on willingness to adopt new tools
notes: Peers’ positive experiences with tools increase openness to adoption
Follows recommendations from technical leads for equipment or software upgrades
Management typically encourages experimentation with new production tools

topic: Primary Tools and Technologies Used in Work

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific software, platforms, or systems used daily
notes: Uses MacBook Pro as primary workstation
Daily use of Avid and Adobe editing software (e.g., Premiere Pro, After Effects)
Employs scriptwriting software for script breakdowns

subtopics.subtopic_description: Essential non-AI tools for workflow
notes: Relies on a sketch pad and pencil for initial storyboarding
Uses email extensively for client and crew communication
Consults physical scripts and production binders on set

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with industry-standard technologies
notes: Highly familiar with Avid and Adobe Creative Suite for editing
Understands basic operation of camera equipment and sound gear
Stays updated on new tools through industry events and peer recommendations

subtopics.subtopic_description: Interoperability or integration issues between tools
notes: Occasionally experiences file compatibility issues between different editing platforms
Manual transfer of storyboards from paper to digital formats can be time-consuming
Scriptwriting software may not always sync seamlessly with production management tools

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with fundamental AI/ML concepts and terminology
notes: Understands basic concepts such as large language models, generative AI, and natural language processing
Aware of AI applications in content generation, editing, and research
Regularly explores how AI can streamline production planning

subtopics.subtopic_description: Names of specific AI software/platforms currently used in work
notes: Has used ChatGPT or similar language models for script ideation and revision
Occasionally tries AI-powered tools in Adobe Creative Suite (e.g., auto-color, sound cleanup)
Tests generative AI platforms for visual inspiration and mood boards

subtopics.subtopic_description: Frequency and purpose of AI tool application (specific use cases)
notes: Uses LLMs regularly for brainstorming, rewriting, or ideation tasks
Applies AI tools occasionally for specific technical tasks (e.g., enhancing audio)
Considers AI for generating alternative visual concepts or prop ideas pre-production

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific examples of AI success and failure experiences (lessons learned)
notes: Found AI helpful for generating script rewrites quickly under tight deadlines
Experienced mixed results when using AI for visual references—sometimes too generic
Learned that AI-generated content still requires significant human curation and adjustment

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of organizational training or peer resources for AI use
notes: Receives informal guidance from technically savvy peers
No formal company-wide AI training program currently available
Relies on online tutorials and industry forums for AI tool learning

topic: AI Interaction Style and Workflow Change

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preferred mode of interaction: independent versus step-by-step collaboration
notes: Prefers independent exploration of AI tools for creative tasks
Uses AI as a brainstorming partner but maintains control over final decisions
Seeks step-by-step collaboration with AI for technical or data-heavy processes

subtopics.subtopic_description: Style of human-AI teaming (e.g., advisor, assistant, co-worker)
notes: Views AI primarily as an assistant for idea generation and administrative support
Occasionally treats AI as an advisor for technical problem-solving
Does not rely on AI for directorial decision-making or final creative calls

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness and openness to adopting new AI-driven workflows
notes: Open to integrating AI into more aspects of pre-production and research
Willing to test AI-driven solutions if they reduce manual workload
Prefers gradual adoption—starting with non-critical tasks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preference for conversational vs. command-based interfaces (communication dynamics)
notes: Prefers conversational interfaces (e.g., chatbots) for brainstorming and creative input
Finds command-based interfaces more suitable for repetitive or technical tasks
Values intuitive, user-friendly AI interfaces that don’t disrupt workflow

subtopics.subtopic_description: Extent to which tasks rely on specialized, tacit domain knowledge
notes: Many production decisions rely heavily on tacit knowledge from years of experience
Script interpretation and talent direction require nuanced understanding of client goals
AI is seen as less capable in areas demanding creative intuition and on-the-fly judgment

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of trust in AI outputs for work tasks and critical decisions
notes: Moderate trust in AI-generated suggestions for administrative and research tasks
Low trust in AI for final creative direction or talent management
Always reviews and validates AI-generated content before use

subtopics.subtopic_description: Ideal balance of human effort and AI automation for specific tasks
notes: Prefers AI automation for repetitive, data-driven, or research-intensive activities
Maintains human control over creative and client-facing decisions
Supports a hybrid workflow where AI augments but does not replace human judgment

subtopics.subtopic_description: Conditions under which high automation is acceptable or threatening
notes: Accepts high automation for routine scheduling, document management, and asset cataloging
Considers high automation threatening if it encroaches on creative or relationship-driven aspects
Wants clear override and review mechanisms for all AI-driven outputs

topic: AI Impact on Skills and Job Security

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived impact of AI on the importance of existing skills (enhanced vs. reduced)
notes: Believes AI enhances efficiency but creative vision and leadership remain paramount
Views communication and client management skills as irreplaceable
Sees technical skills in AI tool usage becoming increasingly important

subtopics.subtopic_description: Emerging skills or new areas of responsibility created by AI
notes: Developing proficiency in prompt engineering for LLMs
Learning to evaluate and curate AI-generated content for quality and suitability
Taking on responsibility for integrating new AI tools into team processes

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of concern about AI replacing specific tasks or the overall role
notes: Minimal concern about AI replacing core creative and leadership responsibilities
Recognizes some administrative and research tasks may be automated
Believes human creativity and team management will remain critical

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of override mechanisms or manual checks for AI-driven processes
notes: Insists on manual review and approval for all AI-generated scripts or visual content
Encourages team to double-check automated outputs before implementation
Keeps all key creative decisions under human oversight

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived change in team or company policies regarding AI adoption
notes: Team is increasingly open to experimenting with AI tools for efficiency
No formal mandate, but gradual shift towards digital and AI-enhanced workflows
Policies emphasize human review and ethical use of AI

subtopics.subtopic_description: General outlook on AI's broader societal and industry impact
notes: Believes AI will streamline many aspects of media production and advertising
Sees potential for AI to democratize creative tools and lower entry barriers
Cautious about the effects of AI on employment and creative originality

subtopics.subtopic_description: Personal beliefs about the ethics and risks of AI in the workplace
notes: Concerned about data privacy and copyright issues with AI-generated content
Believes transparency and disclosure of AI usage are essential
Supports ethical guidelines to prevent misuse of AI in media production

subtopics.subtopic_description: Missing AI tools or features that would be most beneficial in the future
notes: Wants AI tools that can generate highly customized visual references for storyboards
Desires AI-driven assistants for organizing and summarizing complex email threads
Would value an AI-based script breakdown tool that integrates with current workflow

subtopics.subtopic_description: Predicted evolution of their job in the next 5-10 years with AI integration
notes: Expects increased automation in administrative and research tasks
Foresees a shift towards hybrid creative teams where AI augments but doesn’t replace human input
Anticipates new creative opportunities emerging from advanced AI content generation

subtopics.subtopic_description: Concrete steps they would want their organization to take regarding AI strategy
notes: Implement regular training sessions on AI tool usage and best practices
Establish clear guidelines for ethical and transparent AI use
Encourage experimentation with AI while maintaining human oversight on core creative tasks
Invest in pilot projects to test promising AI solutions before wide-scale adoption
